subject: Statistics
title: Kurtosis: Peaks and Heavy Tails
description: A 60-second lesson on Kurtosis: Peaks and Heavy Tails.

hook.text: Two bell curves look identical at first. But one produces far more extreme outliers than the other.

buildup.text: Kurtosis measures how heavy the tails are — how likely extreme values are to appear.

discovery.text: High kurtosis means more surprises. Low kurtosis means data stays near the center with few shocks.

twist.text: Stock markets have high kurtosis. Crashes happen far more often than bell curve models predict.

climax.text: Ignoring heavy tails is dangerous. Models that assume thin tails miss the events that matter most.

punchline.text: The tails are where the surprises live.

quiz.question: What does kurtosis measure?
options: How heavy the tails of a distribution are — the likelihood of extreme values
